title: Neuroplastic Rewiring
definition: The brain's ability to physically change and adapt due to experience and training, leading to permanent changes in neural networks.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The brain can alter itself by learning new things or changing its habits. This is like rewiring a house's electrical system.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Through deliberate training and practice, the brain can undergo structural changes that affect its default processing and perception of reality.
difficulty_levels.expert: Neuroplasticity is the brain's inherent ability to change its neural connections in response to experience. This includes learning new skills, adapting to injury, and altering thought patterns.

extra_fields.insight: The Buddhist path of mindfulness, meditation, and ethical living leads to a rewiring of the brain that results in a shift from a self-centered perception to an impersonal, ownerless view of reality.
